title: Car Washes

narrative.executive_view: NAICS 811192 comprises establishments primarily cleaning, washing or waxing automotive vehicles, including detail shops, mobile washes and self-service washes [S1]. It is a durable consumer service but a weak AI value-migration thesis: the major historic labor-removal event was tunnel and kiosk capital equipment, not AI. A buyer can use AI around member service, marketing, pricing and multi-site planning, but the likely operating gain is small against a modest wage base and format-specific capex, real-estate and local-competition risks.
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: The AI surface is limited to customer support, membership billing/retention, marketing, demand analytics, labor planning and centralized management. Physical vehicle cleaning, equipment maintenance, loading and site supervision remain on site. Mister Car Wash's public model illustrates the distinction: it sells express-exterior washes through kiosks and team members and operates a large subscription program [S2]. Existing unattended or automated tunnels should not be credited as new AI labor replacement.
narrative.value_capture: There is no payer or regulated fee schedule above the operator; a direct-to-consumer site initially retains a cost saving. But price capture is local: an express exterior site with a valuable subscription base can defend price better than a legacy full-service site surrounded by new tunnels. Mister's Unlimited Wash Club represented 76% of its wash sales in 2025, showing the economic importance of subscriptions for a scaled express operator [S2]. The selected distribution and moat inputs retain meaningful competition leakage rather than assuming a subscription business automatically keeps all savings.
narrative.adoption_timing: Current effective AI deployment is low because most potential benefit sits in a thin above-site and customer-administration layer, not the wash itself. A six-year path to 50% of that narrow applicable workflow is reasonable for a regional chain as data, membership systems and centralization mature; it is not a claim that half of car-wash labor will be automated. The absence of a disclosed industrywide AI-adoption series is a material uncertainty.
narrative.consolidation_economics: Consolidation is real but has cooled from its peak and remains format dependent. Car Wash Advisory publishes a 2025 market report based on proprietary transaction experience [S3], and Amplify recorded Driven Brands' agreement to sell its U.S. car-wash business to Whistle Express for $385 million [S4]. A published Q1 2025 small-business table places $1–5M EBITDA express exterior at 5.6x and full service at 4.2x, with $5–10M EBITDA at 7.0x and 5.9x respectively [S5]. These are advisory benchmarks, not directly comparable confidential deals; owner compensation, rent and real estate must be normalized.
narrative.terminal_demand: Demand for paid vehicle washing remains durable because AI does not wash cars and cannot eliminate site convenience, water, equipment and physical cleaning. The terminal issue is not AI substitution but whether an operator owns an attractive trade area, a defendable membership book and a viable lease or real-estate position. Mister's subscription concentration demonstrates that mature express operators have recurring demand, but it also makes retention and churn central rather than automatic [S2].
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: Format heterogeneity is decisive. Express exterior, full-service/flex, self-service, detailing and mobile operators share NAICS 811192 but have very different labor, capex, membership and valuation profiles. The supplied occupational mix is 5-digit 81119 and includes oil-change/lube-shop contamination, so automotive technicians and tire repairers are not treated as car-wash labor opportunity. The target case is a regional express-exterior membership operator; if the available band is instead full-service, self-service or real-estate-heavy, the score and multiples should be rerun.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS, Car Washes Definition
sources.what_it_supports: Defines 811192 as establishments primarily cleaning, washing and/or waxing automotive vehicles and lists detail, mobile and self-service examples.

sources.title: Mister Car Wash 2025 Form 10-K
sources.what_it_supports: Scaled express-exterior formats, subscription economics, kiosks/team-member sales process, and 76% of wash sales from Unlimited Wash Club in 2025.

sources.title: 2025 Car Wash M&A Report
sources.what_it_supports: Confirms a current specialist car-wash M&A report built from proprietary data and transaction experience; detailed report contents are not treated as public disclosed comps.

sources.title: Car Wash Industry Market Update from Amplify
sources.what_it_supports: Reports the February 2025 agreement to sell Driven Brands' U.S. car-wash business to Whistle Express for $385 million.

sources.title: Car Wash EBITDA & Valuation Multiples — 2025 Report
sources.what_it_supports: Published Q1 2025 format- and EBITDA-band valuation benchmark table for car washes, including express exterior and full service.

sources.title: The Carwash M&A Picture in 2025
sources.what_it_supports: Post-2022 financing-cost and multiple-compression context for car-wash M&A.

labor_share.method: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 811192 ($6,105,051,106) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 811192 ($14,968,891,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

occupations.title: First-Line Supervisors of Transportation and Material Moving Workers, Except Aircraft Cargo Handling Supervisors
First-Line Supervisors of Mechanics, Installers, and Repairers

n_band.derivation: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[81]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $89,975 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 811192); class <5: 9,362 firms, avg revenue/firm $236,758 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $37,053 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 2,475 firms, avg revenue/firm $711,100 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $111,287 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 2,110 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,148,854 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $179,796 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 1,542 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,320,900 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $363,221 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 124 firms, avg revenue/firm $10,254,500 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,604,829 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 62 firms, avg revenue/firm $59,966,484 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $9,384,755 -> IN $1-10M band; n_band = 186
n_band.margin_source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: AI addresses a limited administrative and above-site layer. Physical washing, loading, maintenance and site presence remain the labor core; legacy tunnel automation is excluded.
caveats: The supplied 5-digit occupational mix includes oil-change/lube-shop occupations and is a disclosed proxy.
Fractions exclude pre-existing tunnel, gate and kiosk automation that is not AI.

breakdown_by_role.rationale: Vehicle cleaning/prep is physical work; only minor task coordination is assistable.
Payments and some guest workflow can be automated, while loading, guiding and service remain physical.
This proxy occupation is largely sibling-code contamination; physical equipment upkeep is not AI replacement.

breakdown_by_role.role: First-Line Supervisors of Transportation and Material Moving Workers, Except Aircraft Cargo Handling Supervisors
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Scheduling and labor planning can be assisted; site supervision remains.
Multi-site reporting, pricing analysis and planning can be assisted, but operating leadership persists.
Kiosk, app and membership workflow can replace some transactions; sales and exception handling persist.

breakdown_by_role.role: First-Line Supervisors of Mechanics, Installers, and Repairers
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Maintenance supervision is mainly physical and is a 5-digit proxy.
Tire work is sibling-code contamination and physical; do not infer a car-wash AI channel.
Membership service, chargebacks and standard customer contacts are highly assistable with human escalation.
Back-office record handling and bookkeeping are a clean automation surface.
Unlisted marketing, HR and administrative work has partial AI exposure.

pi_dist.rationale: Direct retail pricing permits initial retention of savings, but local tunnel supply and membership discounting can compete away a material share.
caveats: No trade-area-level supply, pricing or churn data are available for the target EBITDA band.

pi_moat.rationale: Membership habits and location convenience can create retention, but entry and format competition make moats local rather than industrywide.
caveats: Mister is a scaled public benchmark, not evidence that a regional target has the same subscription density.

t50_years.rationale: Only a narrow applicable workflow remains after existing tunnel and kiosk automation, so meaningful AI penetration is gradual and data-system dependent.
caveats: No representative current AI-adoption survey for NAICS 811192 was located; this is a bounded judgment.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: Scaled operators use technology-rich subscription and point-of-sale workflows, but that is not equivalent to broad effective AI deployment.
caveats: Do not equate kiosks, gates or tunnel equipment with AI adoption.

historical_analogs.value: Express-tunnel, kiosk and subscription rollout is a cautionary analogue: it changed labor and retention through capital equipment and recurring billing, not through AI; AI should diffuse only through the remaining central workflow layer.
historical_analogs.rationale: The analogue constrains the thesis by separating mature automation from new AI value.
caveats: This is not evidence of a measured generative-AI adoption curve.

owners_60plus_pct.rationale: A fragmented market plausibly contains many legacy owners, but no representative 811192 owner-age dataset was found.
caveats: Validate owner age, real-estate ownership and format locally before underwriting.

succession_shortage_documented.rationale: The current specialist M&A report and a 2025 strategic sale document active transaction supply and buyer interest, supporting but not quantifying succession opportunity.
caveats: This is not a nationwide owner-survey result.

active_consolidators.rationale: Conservative count of visible strategic and sponsor-backed consolidator platforms, not all buyers or individual transactions.
caveats: Buyer activity differs substantially by region, format and real-estate ownership.

buy_mult.rationale: Selected between the published $1–5M EBITDA full-service 4.2x and express-exterior 5.6x figures to reflect an underwritten regional mixed-quality target and post-peak financing conditions.
caveats: Published advisory benchmark, not confidential deal comps; EBITDAR and owned real estate require separate treatment.

exit_mult.rationale: Assumes format conversion, a defensible membership book and normalized rent can move a regional target toward the express benchmark without assuming peak-era multiples.
caveats: Exit depends on local supply, membership churn and real-estate/lease terms; no multiple expansion is assumed outside the published range.

pricing_structure.value: Direct-to-consumer single washes and monthly unlimited-wash subscriptions; no payer fee schedule, but local competition determines price retention.
pricing_structure.rationale: Mister's filing describes UWC monthly subscriptions and point-of-sale wash transactions as distinct revenue streams.
caveats: Mister's 76% UWC sales share is a scaled-public-company benchmark, not an industry average.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: The low AI-replaceable share and six-year adoption time prevent a material AI-driven EBITDA step-up; the thesis does not credit existing tunnels as AI.

terminal_value.justification: Physical vehicle cleaning and convenient local service remain paid demand; the key investment risk is competition and asset economics, not AI replacing the service.

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: The 5-digit occupational mix is explicitly a proxy with oil-change/lube contamination; no automotive technician or tire-repair role is used to claim car-wash AI value.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: Direct retail pricing supports pi_dist, while local price competition is explicitly treated as a capture risk; it is not used to manufacture terminal-demand weakness.

dataset_mismatch.description: Role mix is 5-digit 81119, which includes 811191 oil-change/lube shops; supplied labor share combines 2024 wages with 2022 receipts.
sensitivity: Removing sibling-code mechanical occupations lowers apparent physical-maintenance exposure but does not create an AI labor channel.
A larger express-exterior share lowers labor intensity and makes AI uplift smaller in absolute dollars.

market_boundary.dominant_subsegment: Regional express-exterior operator with an established membership program
market_boundary.selection_basis: This is the format where the public subscription benchmark and $1–10M EBITDA valuation table are most relevant; non-express formats receive explicit multiple and labor caveats.

top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: The narrow remaining AI surface means slower adoption sharply limits the already modest value contribution.
Format mix and the 5-digit occupation proxy can materially change the AI-labor estimate.
Format, membership quality, trade-area supply, rent and real estate can dominate returns over any AI operating gain.

reviewer_note: All six listed driving URLs were reopened on 2026-07-21. Multiples are supported by a published advisory benchmark and marked low-confidence rather than presented as disclosed private transaction comps.
